title: Bharti Airtel, HAL, among 7 stocks that have upside potential of up 28%. Do you own any?
summary: Amid rising geopolitical tensions, surging crude oil prices and continued FII outflows, ICICI Securities has identified seven largecap stocks with strong upside potential. The brokerage believes these sector leaders, backed by robust fundamentals and long-term growth visibility, could outperform despite volatile market conditions and uncertain investor sentiment.

title: Stock Market Holiday 2026: Are NSE, BSE closed today for Bakri Id? Check market closing dates list
summary: Indian stock markets will be closed on May 28 for Bakri Id, marking another trading holiday in 2026. Following this, seven more market closures are scheduled throughout the year, including Muharram, Ganesh Chaturthi, and Diwali. MCX will have a partial closure, while NCDEX will be fully shut.

title: InvITs industry pushes for easier M&A rules to boost investor interest
summary: India's InvITs sector, managing ₹7 lakh crore in assets, is urging regulators for simpler M&A rules. Industry leaders want change of control guidelines to match those for listed companies. This aims to attract more investors by streamlining acquisition processes. Current rules can make buyers acquire more units than intended.

title: Adani Power surges past Infosys in market capitalisation
summary: Adani Power has surpassed Infosys in market capitalization, becoming the 11th most valuable company on Indian bourses. This shift is driven by Adani Power's 68% stock surge in 2026, while Infosys experienced a nearly 29% decline. Investors favor Adani Power due to rising energy demand, whereas concerns over AI's impact on traditional IT services have affected Infosys.

title: Nifty Midcap 100 scales fresh peak, analysts see more upside ahead
summary: The Nifty Midcap 100 index reached a record high for the fourth consecutive session, driven by strong corporate earnings and promising growth outlooks. Analysts anticipate this momentum to continue, with potential for 20% gains in the medium term, as midcap and smallcap stocks attract significant investor interest.

title: States under fiscal strain amid high welfare spend: Morgan Stanley
summary: States are facing fiscal pressure as revenue growth slows and welfare spending remains high, leading to elevated deficits. Morgan Stanley reports state fiscal deficits are projected to stay at 3.2% of GSDP through FY27, while the consolidated fiscal deficit is expected to rise to 7.9%.

title: Non-banks increase microfinance share amid muted demand
summary: The overall microfinance gross loan portfolio stood at ₹3.34 lakh crore at the end of April, down from ₹3.39 lakh crore a month ago, said the credit information company.

title: HDFC Bank denies allegations of disguising interest payout
summary: HDFC Bank stands resolutely against accusations of misconduct as an investigative report alleges that it masked differential interest payments to MSRDC as part of its marketing budget. The bank emphasizes its stringent internal controls and refutes any speculation of wrongdoing.

title: Avaada seeks $800 million to refinance Brookfield Asset Management debt
summary: Avaada Group is raising $800 million to refinance existing debt. This move aims to reduce finance costs before the group's planned public market listing. The funds will come from offshore loans and local bonds. This refinancing will replace part of the debt from Brookfield Asset Management. Avaada is expanding its renewable energy and manufacturing businesses.

title: AkzoNobel rejects €12.5 billion Nippon Paint, Sherwin-Williams takeover offer
summary: Dulux paint maker AkzoNobel has rejected a massive takeover offer. Rivals Nippon Paint and Sherwin-Williams proposed a €12.5 billion deal. AkzoNobel stated the offer undervalued its business and lacked certainty. The company board continues to back its planned merger with Axalta. Shareholders will vote on the Axalta deal soon. This rejection sent AkzoNobel shares soaring.

title: IDFC First Bank receives Rs 514.82 crore insurance payout on microfinance defaults, expects full provision write-back
summary: IDFC First Bank has successfully secured a substantial allocation of over Rs 500 crore from the Credit Guarantee Fund for Micro Units. This funding is targeted at eligible microfinance loans and is expected to enhance the bank's financial performance by allowing it to reverse previously set aside provisions for defaults.

title: Oil Rebounds as US Hits Hormuz Targets and Kuwait Repels Drones
summary: Oil advanced, following a drop of more than 5% on Wednesday, on fresh strikes in the Persian Gulf, while Washington and Tehran remained at odds over how to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Gold Extends Losses as US Strikes Dampen Iran Peace Talk Hopes
summary: Gold extended a two-day decline as fresh US airstrikes on an Iranian military base risked derailing peace talks and kept inflation risks high.

title: Gold, Silver prices tumble amid fresh US strikes on Iran, strong US dollar; should investors worry now?
summary: Gold is trading near $4,450, and silver around $72 to $78.50, as rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions impact inflation outlook and investor sentiment.

title: Q4 results 2026: Ashok Leyland to Bharat Dynamics among companies to declare Q4 results today; full list here
summary: Q4 results 2026: Around 400 companies will declare their Q4 results 2026 today. Check full list, along with Q4 preview.

title: Oil prices rise 2% on fresh US strikes on Iran; Brent crude at $96/bbl
summary: Brent crude futures advanced $1.90, or 2.02%, to $96.19 per barrel, while the more actively traded August contract rose $1.64, or 1.78%, to $93.89 per barrel

title: Asian markets today: Nikkei 225, Kospi fall amid US-Iran negotiations
summary: Asian markets today: Japan’s Nikkei 225 dropped 0.76%, with the Topix also trading 0.71% lower. Here's how other Asian markets are performing today, along with US-Iran war update.

title: Investors are nervous about a sky-high market. Why stocks may keep rising.
summary: Goldman Sachs Asset Management experts say investors should use dividend stocks and buffer ETFs to manage risk rather than fleeing equities..

title: Stock market holiday: Are BSE, NSE closed for Bakrid? Check full 2026 trading holiday list
summary: The Indian stock market will remain closed today, May 28, 2026, for Bakri Id. Trading will resume in the next regular session. Check the complete 2026 stock market holiday calendar here.

title: Selling Essar guarantees will be a long battle for lenders
summary: Experts said while it is the best thing for Essar lenders to do, buyers of these guarantees will not pay enough to cover banks' dues.

title: Medtech exporter Poly Medicure bets on high-tech pivot and acquisitions to fend off macro headwinds
summary: The medtech major expects its growth rate to more than double in FY27 as it pivots from low-cost consumables to high-value equipment to counter Chinese competition and rising input costs.

title: PhysicsWallah reins in K-12 ambitions as profitability takes priority
summary: The edtech major is sharpening its focus on profitability across its core online and offline education businesses.

title: Annapurna Yojana: Unable to fill enrolment form online or offline? Here's how beneficiaries will get help
summary: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari launched the 'Annapurna Bhandar' scheme, offering  ₹3,000 monthly assistance to eligible women. Applications can be submitted online or offline, with verification ensuring benefits reach the right recipients.&nbsp;

title: EPFO 3.0: Want to withdraw 100% of your PF money? Check the latest withdrawal rules
summary: EPFO subscribers will soon be able to access their provident fund savings directly through UPI, reducing paperwork and waiting times. Members can withdraw 50% to 75% of their EPF balance. The new system aims for a paperless experience, with testing completed and rollout expected soon.

title: US degree now costs more than a 2BHK in Bengaluru and Gurgaon – How parents are prioritising
summary: Indian parents face emotional and financial challenges sending children abroad for education, with costs rising to  ₹5 crore by 2036 due to rupee depreciation and tuition hikes. Despite high expenses, 90% still prefer overseas studies, relying on long-term investments to ease the financial burden.

title: 8th Pay Commission latest update: Big salary hike unlikely, why Centre may settle for a moderate pay revision
summary: Speculation about the 8th Pay Commission grows as central government employees await long-promised salary revisions. Reports indicate the Centre may not meet all union demands, including a higher fitment factor, raising doubts about substantial pay hikes amid rising inflation pressures.

title: Bank holiday today, May 28: Are SBI, HDFC Bank and other banks open or closed for Bakrid/Eid al-Adha?
summary: Most banks will be closed on May 28, 2026, for Bakrid/Eid al-Adha, a gazetted public holiday. Central government offices will also observe this day off, replacing the previously scheduled holiday on May 27.

title: Nio surges 9% after releasing first flagship EV in more than two years
summary: Chinese electric car company Nio has launched two lower-priced brands in the last two years to broaden its customer base in a sluggish Chinese consumer market.
